titleStmt.title: Letter from B.J. Collingwood to Edward O’Farrell, 2 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: B.J. Collingwood

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from B.J Collingwood, Professor of Pathology and Bacteriology at University College Dublin, to Sir. Edward O’Farrell (c. 1857-1926), the assistant under-secretary, Dublin Castle.
As a person licensed to carry out experiments on animals, Collingwood was obliged to furnish an annual Return of Experiments. In this letter he is submits his Return together with an apology for its lateness due to work pressures.
Enclosed is a letter from Edward O’Farrell to B.J. Collingwood, dated 22 February 1916, in which the Return of Experiments is requested.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5781

correspAction.placeName: Adelaide House, Earlsfort Terrace, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Adelaide House
Carlsfort Terrace
Dublin Ap 2 1916 Dear Sir, I am very sorry that by an
oversight I have ommitted to send
this earlier. I have been
completed overwhelmed with
work at University & also from
Military. faithfully yrs B. Hollingwood
2654
IMMEDIATE. Dublin Castle, 22nd February 1916 Sir, I am directed by the Chief Secretary to request that you will state on
the enclosed form :— (1) The number of experiments on living animals performed by you in
the year ended 31st December ;ast, under licence granted to you
under the Act 39 and 40 Vic, cap. 77, or any special certificate
you may have held during that year: (2) The general purpose of the experiments; and (3) The number of those experiments in which there was reason to
believe that any appreciable pain was inflicted, specifying the
kind of animal used and stating in general terms the nature of
the experiment occasioned pain. All experiments under Certificate C must be performed with anaesthetics,
and it should be stated in your Return that the animals used were killed before
recovery from anaethesia. I have also to request that you will furnish in Table 4 of the form
enclosed a complete list of any writings based upon the experimental work which
you may have published during the past year, giving the full title of each
paper with an exact reference to any periodical in which it may have appeared,
and the name of any Society to which it may have been communicated, and
that if you have not already done so, you will forward copies of such papers
for the information of the Chief Secretary. As it is desirable that the preparation of the Parliamentary Return
relating to Experiments on Living Animals should be undertaken at once, you
Return should be furnished within one week. Your licence under the Act will expire d on the 28th instant Feb,1915, . If you desire
its renewal an application to that effect should be made stating the grounds.
The licence should at the same time be forwarded. I am,
SIR,
Your obedient Servant E O'Connell B.J Collingwood, Esqr MD,
Adelaide House
Earlsfort Terrace,
Dublin
